layer1.standard: Thickened fascia of obturator internus forming a linear origin for levator ani.
layer1.actions: Provides structural origin for iliococcygeus (part of levator ani).
layer1.movements: Not contractile. Passive structural anchor for pelvic diaphragm.
layer1.pri: R arcuate tendon is not a primary PRI corrective. Only L arcuate tendon is assigned a color family.

layer1.standard: Lateral rotation of the hip. When hip is flexed beyond 90°, assists abduction.
layer1.actions: Hip lateral rotation (open chain), hip abduction assist (flexed hip).
layer1.movements: Femoral external rotation, hip joint stabilization.
layer1.pri: R obturator internus is not a primary PRI corrective. Only L OI is targeted for outlet abduction (IsP ER).

layer1.standard: Pulls coccyx anteriorly, supports posterior pelvic floor.
layer1.actions: Coccyx flexion, posterior pelvic floor support.
layer1.movements: Coccyx flexion, pelvic floor stabilization.
layer1.pri: R coccygeus → R SI IR (posterior outlet closing). Corrective in L AIC.

layer2.laic: R coccygeus assists R SI IR. Facilitated alongside R glute max and R piriformis in Step 4.

layer3.treatment: Step 4: R coccygeus + R piriformis + R glute max. HALT 4/5.

layer1.standard: Supports pelvic viscera, elevates pelvic floor, stabilizes coccyx. Part of levator ani.

proximal: Arcuate tendon (tendinous arch of levator ani)

layer1.actions: Pelvic floor elevation, visceral support.
layer1.movements: Pelvic floor elevation, coccyx stabilization.
layer1.pri: R iliococcygeus is not a primary PRI corrective. Only L iliococcygeus is targeted for outlet abduction.

layer1.standard: Maintains anorectal angle (~90°), assists fecal continence. U-shaped sling around rectum.

distal: Loops posterior to rectum (U-shaped sling — no bony distal attachment)

layer1.actions: Maintains anorectal angle, assists fecal continence.
layer1.movements: Anorectal angle maintenance, rectal support.
layer1.pri: L puborectalis → sagittal plane outlet muscle. Assists L anterior outlet IsP ER (opening).

layer2.laic: Part of sagittal pelvic floor restoration. Facilitated in Step 2 alongside L OI and L iliococcygeus via L adductors.
layer2.pathology: Descended/inhibited in L AIC (left outlet in IsP IR). Bilateral inhibition in B PEC.

layer3.treatment: Step 2: L puborectalis + L pubococcygeus + L iliococcygeus + L obturator internus (via L adductors). HALT 1/5.

layer1.standard: Fibrous band between anus and coccyx tip. Anchors posterior pelvic floor musculature.
layer1.actions: Anchors posterior pelvic floor to coccyx. Not contractile.
layer1.movements: Structural anchor for pelvic floor musculature.
layer1.pri: Outlet structural bridge. Midline reference for pelvic floor orientation.

layer2.laic: Midline reference for sagittal pelvic floor position.

layer3.treatment: Not directly targeted. Structural landmark for pelvic floor orientation.

layer1.standard: Thickened fascia of obturator internus forming a linear origin for levator ani.
layer1.actions: Provides structural origin for iliococcygeus (part of levator ani).
layer1.movements: Not contractile. Passive structural anchor for pelvic diaphragm.
layer1.pri: L arcuate tendon connects L obturator internus fascial plane to L pelvic floor origin. Key anatomical bridge.

layer2.laic: Anatomical bridge for L pelvic floor muscle activation sequences. Links L OI to L iliococcygeus.

layer3.treatment: Not directly targeted. Awareness landmark for pelvic floor anatomy.

layer1.standard: Hip extension, knee flexion, tibial internal rotation.
layer1.actions: Hip extension, knee flexion, tibial internal rotation.
layer1.movements: Posterior pelvic tilt (via hip extension), knee flexion, tibial IR.
layer1.pri: L hamstrings assist posterior pelvic tilt. Not a primary PRI facilitation target.

layer2.laic: L hamstrings assist corrective posterior tilt in 90-90 hip lift positions but are not primary facilitation targets.

layer3.treatment: Indirectly trained. 90-90 hip lift activities use hamstrings for posterior tilt.

layer1.standard: Lateral rotation of the hip (open chain). When hip is flexed beyond 90°, assists abduction.

distal: Medial surface of greater trochanter (exits via lesser sciatic foramen)

layer1.actions: Hip lateral rotation (open chain), hip abduction assist (flexed hip).
layer1.movements: Femoral external rotation, hip joint stabilization.
layer1.pri: L OI → L IsP ER (outlet abduction). In closed chain, pulls L ischial tuberosity laterally, widening the L outlet. THE key corrective muscle in L AIC.

layer2.laic: L OI → L IsP ER. Opens left outlet, ascends pelvic diaphragm. Inhibited in L AIC. Facilitated via L adductors — their contraction activates L OI in closed chain.
layer2.pathology: Inhibited in L AIC (left outlet in IsP IR). Bilateral inhibition in B PEC and Patho PEC.

layer3.treatment: Step 2: L OI + L pelvic floor (puborectalis, pubococcygeus, iliococcygeus) via L adductors. HALT 1/5. Exercise: R Sidelying Respiratory L Adductor Pull Back with Balloon.

layer1.standard: Hip extension, lateral rotation, abduction (upper fibers), adduction (lower fibers).
layer1.actions: Hip extension, femoral lateral rotation, abduction (upper fibers), adduction (lower fibers).
layer1.movements: Hip extension, femoral ER, pelvic stabilization in stance.
layer1.pri: L glute max is not a primary PRI corrective. Only R glute max is targeted (inlet and outlet).

layer1.standard: Supports pelvic viscera, elevates pelvic floor, stabilizes coccyx. Part of levator ani.

proximal: Arcuate tendon (tendinous arch of levator ani)

layer1.actions: Pelvic floor elevation, visceral support, coccyx stabilization.
layer1.movements: Pelvic floor elevation.
layer1.pri: L iliococcygeus → L outlet abduction. Pulls L ischial tuberosity laterally, assists L OI in widening the L outlet (IsP ER) and ascending L pelvic diaphragm.

layer2.laic: Works with L obturator internus for L pelvic diaphragm ascension. Inhibited in L AIC (L outlet in IsP IR).
layer2.pathology: Inhibited in L AIC. Bilateral inhibition in B PEC.

layer3.treatment: Step 2: Facilitated alongside L OI via L adductors. HALT 1/5.

layer1.standard: Supports pelvic viscera, assists urinary and fecal continence. Part of levator ani.
layer1.actions: Pelvic floor elevation and compression, visceral support, continence.
layer1.movements: Pelvic floor elevation, urinary and fecal continence.
layer1.pri: L pubococcygeus → sagittal plane outlet muscle. Assists L anterior outlet IsP ER (opening) and posterior pelvic tilt.

layer2.laic: Assists sagittal correction of pelvic position. Part of L outlet restoration in Step 2.
layer2.pathology: Descended/inhibited in L AIC. Bilateral inhibition in B PEC.

layer3.treatment: Step 2: Facilitated alongside L OI, L iliococcygeus, L puborectalis via L adductors. HALT 1/5.

layer1.standard: Maintains anorectal angle (~90°), assists fecal continence. U-shaped sling around rectum.

distal: Loops posterior to rectum (U-shaped sling — no bony distal attachment)

layer1.actions: Maintains anorectal angle, assists fecal continence.
layer1.movements: Anorectal angle maintenance, rectal support.
layer1.pri: L puborectalis → sagittal plane outlet muscle. Assists L anterior outlet IsP ER (opening).

layer2.laic: Part of sagittal pelvic floor restoration. Facilitated in Step 2 alongside L OI and L iliococcygeus via L adductors.
layer2.pathology: Descended/inhibited in L AIC. Bilateral inhibition in B PEC.

layer3.treatment: Step 2: L pelvic floor muscles via L adductors. HALT 1/5.

layer1.standard: Supports pelvic viscera, assists urinary and fecal continence. Part of levator ani.
layer1.actions: Pelvic floor elevation and compression, visceral support, continence.
layer1.movements: Pelvic floor elevation, urinary and fecal continence.
layer1.pri: L pubococcygeus → sagittal plane outlet muscle. Assists L anterior outlet IsP ER (opening).

layer2.laic: Assists sagittal correction of pelvic position. Facilitated in Step 2.
layer2.pathology: Descended/inhibited in L AIC. Bilateral inhibition in B PEC.

layer3.treatment: Step 2: L pelvic floor muscles via L adductors. HALT 1/5.

layer1.standard: Thickened fascia of obturator internus forming a linear origin for levator ani.
layer1.actions: Provides structural origin for iliococcygeus (part of levator ani).
layer1.movements: Not contractile. Passive structural anchor for pelvic diaphragm.
layer1.pri: L arcuate tendon connects L OI fascial plane to L pelvic floor origin. Key anatomical bridge.

layer2.laic: Anatomical bridge for L pelvic floor activation sequences. Links L OI to L iliococcygeus.

layer3.treatment: Not directly targeted. Awareness landmark for pelvic floor anatomy.

layer1.standard: Supports pelvic viscera, elevates pelvic floor, stabilizes coccyx. Part of levator ani.

proximal: Arcuate tendon (tendinous arch of levator ani)

layer1.actions: Pelvic floor elevation, visceral support, coccyx stabilization.
layer1.movements: Pelvic floor elevation.
layer1.pri: L iliococcygeus → L outlet abduction (IsP ER). Assists L OI in widening L outlet and ascending L pelvic diaphragm.

layer2.laic: Works with L obturator internus for L pelvic diaphragm ascension. Inhibited in L AIC.
layer2.pathology: Inhibited in L AIC. Bilateral inhibition in B PEC.

layer3.treatment: Step 2: Facilitated alongside L OI via L adductors. HALT 1/5.

layer1.standard: Lateral rotation of the hip (open chain). When hip is flexed beyond 90°, assists abduction.

distal: Medial surface of greater trochanter (exits via lesser sciatic foramen)

layer1.actions: Hip lateral rotation (open chain), hip abduction assist (flexed hip).
layer1.movements: Femoral external rotation, hip joint stabilization.
layer1.pri: L OI → L IsP ER (outlet abduction). In closed chain, pulls L ischial tuberosity laterally. THE key corrective muscle in L AIC.

layer2.laic: L OI → L IsP ER. Opens left outlet, ascends pelvic diaphragm. Inhibited in L AIC. Facilitated via L adductors.
layer2.pathology: Inhibited in L AIC (left outlet in IsP IR). Bilateral inhibition in B PEC and Patho PEC.

layer3.treatment: Step 2: L OI + L pelvic floor via L adductors. HALT 1/5. Exercise: R Sidelying Respiratory L Adductor Pull Back with Balloon.

layer1.standard: Pulls coccyx anteriorly, supports posterior pelvic floor.
layer1.actions: Coccyx flexion, posterior pelvic floor support.
layer1.movements: Coccyx flexion, pelvic floor stabilization.
layer1.pri: L coccygeus is not a primary PRI corrective. Only R coccygeus is targeted (R SI IR, posterior outlet closing).

layer1.standard: Lateral rotation of the hip (when extended). Hip abduction. When hip flexed >60°, may assist medial rotation.
layer1.actions: Hip lateral rotation (extended), hip abduction, hip stabilization.
layer1.movements: Femoral external rotation, hip stabilization.
layer1.pri: L piriformis is not a primary PRI corrective. Only R piriformis is targeted (R SI IR assist).

layer1.standard: Fibrous band between anus and coccyx tip. Anchors posterior pelvic floor musculature.
layer1.actions: Anchors posterior pelvic floor to coccyx. Not contractile.
layer1.movements: Structural anchor for pelvic floor musculature.
layer1.pri: Outlet structural bridge. Midline reference for pelvic floor orientation.

layer2.laic: Midline reference for sagittal pelvic floor position.

layer3.treatment: Not directly targeted. Structural landmark for pelvic floor orientation.

layer1.standard: Supports pelvic viscera, elevates pelvic floor, stabilizes coccyx. Part of levator ani.

proximal: Arcuate tendon (tendinous arch of levator ani)

layer1.actions: Pelvic floor elevation, visceral support.
layer1.movements: Pelvic floor elevation, coccyx stabilization.
layer1.pri: R iliococcygeus is not a primary PRI corrective. Only L iliococcygeus is targeted for outlet abduction.

layer1.standard: Lateral rotation of the hip. When hip is flexed beyond 90°, assists abduction.
layer1.actions: Hip lateral rotation (open chain), hip abduction assist (flexed hip).
layer1.movements: Femoral external rotation, hip joint stabilization.
layer1.pri: R obturator internus is not a primary PRI corrective. Only L OI is targeted for outlet abduction (IsP ER).

layer1.standard: Pulls coccyx anteriorly, supports posterior pelvic floor.
layer1.actions: Coccyx flexion, posterior pelvic floor support.
layer1.movements: Coccyx flexion, pelvic floor stabilization.
layer1.pri: R coccygeus → R SI IR (posterior outlet closing). Corrective in L AIC.

layer2.laic: R coccygeus assists R SI IR. Facilitated alongside R glute max and R piriformis.

layer3.treatment: Step 4: R coccygeus + R piriformis + R glute max. HALT 4/5.

layer1.standard: Lateral rotation of the hip (when extended). Hip abduction. When hip flexed >60°, may assist medial rotation.
layer1.actions: Hip lateral rotation (extended), hip abduction, hip stabilization.
layer1.movements: Femoral external rotation, hip stabilization.
layer1.pri: R piriformis → R SI IR assist (posterior outlet closing). Corrective in L AIC.

layer2.laic: R piriformis = corrective (closes R posterior outlet). Also restricts ADT when shortened bilaterally.
layer2.pathology: Overactive bilaterally in compensated patterns. R side overactive in L AIC. Hypertonic bilateral in B PEC.

layer3.treatment: Step 4: with R glute max + R coccygeus. HALT 4/5. Inhibited through positioning in early steps.

layer1.standard: Hip extension, ER, abduction (upper fibers).
layer1.attachments: Posterior ilium, sacrum, coccyx, thoracolumbar fascia → IT band and gluteal tuberosity of femur.
layer1.actions: Hip extension, external rotation, abduction (upper fibers), adduction (lower fibers).
layer1.movements: Hip extension, femoral ER, pelvic stabilization in stance.
layer1.pri: L glute max is not a primary PRI corrective. Only R glute max is violet (Internal Rotation).

layer1.standard: Pulls coccyx forward, supports pelvic floor.
layer1.attachments: Ischial spine → lateral margins of coccyx and S5.
layer1.actions: Pulls coccyx anteriorly, supports posterior pelvic floor.
layer1.movements: Coccyx flexion, pelvic floor stabilization.
layer1.pri: R coccygeus → R SI IR (posterior outlet closing). Internal rotation family.

layer2.laic: R coccygeus assists R SI IR. Bilateral coccygeus hypertonic in B PEC.

layer3.treatment: Release through pelvic floor relaxation. Not directly facilitated.

layer1.standard: Femoral ER (open-chain), pelvic diaphragm ascension (closed-chain).
layer1.attachments: Inner surface of obturator membrane and surrounding bone → medial surface of greater trochanter.
layer1.actions: Hip ER (open chain), pelvic diaphragm ascension and IsP ER (closed chain).
layer1.movements: Femoral external rotation, outlet abduction (ischial tuberosities apart).
layer1.pri: L obturator internus → L IsP ER (outlet abduction). Green (Frontal Abduction). THE key corrective muscle.

layer2.laic: L OI → L IsP ER. Opens left outlet, ascends pelvic diaphragm. Inhibited in L AIC. Facilitation restores outlet abduction.
layer2.pathology: Implicated in L AIC (inhibited), B PEC (bilateral inhibition), Patho PEC.

layer3.treatment: Step 2: L obturator internus + L pelvic floor. HALT 1/5 → 2/5. Key exercise: left sidelying knee-apart with balloon.

layer1.standard: Hip flexion, anterior pelvic tilt, stabilizes femoral head in acetabulum.

distal: Lesser trochanter of femur (shared tendon with psoas)

layer1.actions: Hip flexion, anterior pelvic tilt, femoral head stabilization.
layer1.movements: Hip flexion, pelvic anterior tilt, assists lateral rotation of femur.
layer1.pri: PRI distinguishes proximal vs. distal iliacus. Distal iliacus acts on the femur (part of L AIC chain — pathological). Proximal iliacus in closed chain pulls ilium medially = inlet adduction (IP IR) — corrective. PRI calls proximal iliacus 'anterior glute med' (confusing — it is still iliacus).
layer1.chain: Anterior chain — AIC component (distal) / inlet adduction (proximal)

layer2.laic: Distal L iliacus pulls L ilium into anterior tilt (L IP ER) — part of L AIC problem. Proximal L iliacus adducts L inlet (IP IR) — corrective. Facilitated in Step 3.
layer2.pathology: Distal iliacus overactive in L AIC (drives anterior tilt). Proximal iliacus inhibited.

layer3.treatment: Proximal iliacus: Step 3 with L gluteus medius. L posterior inlet (IS ER / counter-nutation). HALT 2/5.

layer1.standard: Hip flexion, abduction, lateral rotation. Knee flexion, medial rotation of tibia.
layer1.actions: Hip flexion, abduction, lateral rotation. Knee flexion, tibial medial rotation.
layer1.movements: Assists 'tailor's position' (hip flexion + abduction + ER). Crosses hip and knee.
layer1.pri: R sartorius → R IP ER (R anterior inlet opening). Assists R rectus femoris in advancing R knee. Corrective in L AIC.

layer2.laic: R sartorius = corrective. Assists R inlet opening (IP ER) in Step 1.

layer3.treatment: Step 1: R sartorius + R rectus femoris. HALT 0/5. Exercise: 90-90 Supported Left Hip Shift with Right Rectus Femoris and Sartorius.

layer1.standard: Hip flexion, knee extension. Only quadriceps head crossing the hip joint. The direct (straight) head originates from the AIIS.
layer1.actions: Hip flexion, knee extension. Biarticular muscle.
layer1.movements: Hip flexion (anterior pelvic tilt), knee extension.
layer1.pri: R rectus femoris → R IP ER (R anterior inlet opening, flex/abd/ER). R = corrective (opens R inlet, Step 1). L = pathological (L inlet already open in L AIC).

layer2.laic: R rectus femoris = CORRECTIVE (advances R knee, opens R inlet). L rectus femoris = shortened/ON in L AIC (L inlet already in IP ER).
layer2.pathology: L rectus femoris shortened (ON) in L AIC. In B PEC: bilateral rectus femoris lengthened (both sides OFF).

layer3.treatment: Step 1: R rectus femoris + R sartorius. HALT 0/5. Exercise: 90-90 Supported Left Hip Shift with Right Rectus Femoris and Sartorius.

layer1.standard: Hip flexion, knee extension. The reflected (indirect) head originates from the superior acetabular rim and hip joint capsule.

distal: Joins direct head → tibial tuberosity via patellar ligament

layer1.actions: Hip flexion, knee extension. Stabilizes hip joint capsule superiorly. Biarticular muscle.
layer1.movements: Hip flexion (anterior pelvic tilt), knee extension.
layer1.pri: R rectus femoris → R IP ER (R anterior inlet opening). R = corrective (Step 1). L = pathological (L inlet already open in L AIC). Same PRI role as direct head.

layer2.laic: R rectus femoris = CORRECTIVE. L rectus femoris = shortened/ON in L AIC.
layer2.pathology: L rectus femoris shortened (ON) in L AIC. In B PEC: bilateral lengthened (both sides OFF).

layer3.treatment: Step 1: R rectus femoris + R sartorius. HALT 0/5.

layer1.standard: IO: ipsilateral trunk rotation, lateral flexion, abdominal compression. TA: abdominal compression, forced expiration, lumbar spine stabilization.

proximal: IO: thoracolumbar fascia, anterior iliac crest, lateral inguinal ligament
TA: thoracolumbar fascia, iliac crest, inguinal ligament, costal cartilages 7–12
distal: IO: ribs 10–12, linea alba, pubic crest

layer1.actions: IO: ipsilateral trunk rotation, lateral flexion, abdominal compression. TA: abdominal compression, forced expiration, lumbar stabilization.
layer1.movements: Core stabilization, forced expiration, abdominal wall tension.
layer1.pri: L IO/TA → conduit between respiratory diaphragm and pelvic inlet. Restore ZOA (zone of apposition). Promote inlet extension (IP IR) and posterior pelvic tilt.

layer2.laic: L IO/TA weak in L AIC. Must restore for inlet extension and diaphragm coupling. The 'conduit' between the two diaphragms.
layer2.pathology: L IO weak in L AIC. Bilateral IO/TA long and weak in B PEC and Patho PEC.

layer3.treatment: Used throughout all hierarchies. Exercises: Standing Wall Supported Reach, Modified All Four Belly Lift, All Four Belly Lift, Wall and Chair Supported IO/TA.

layer1.standard: Hip abduction (all fibers), medial rotation (anterior fibers), lateral rotation (posterior fibers). Key frontal plane pelvic stabilizer in single-leg stance.

proximal: External surface of ilium between anterior and posterior gluteal lines

layer1.actions: Hip abduction, femoral IR (anterior fibers), femoral ER (posterior fibers), pelvic stabilization in single-leg stance.
layer1.movements: Frontal plane pelvic stability, hip abduction, femoral internal rotation (anterior fibers).
layer1.pri: L gluteus medius → L IS ER (counter-nutation). Pulls L ilium laterally relative to sacrum, closing L posterior inlet. Not the same as PRI's 'anterior glute med' (which is proximal iliacus).

layer2.laic: L glute med = corrective. Facilitates L IS ER (counter-nutation) with L proximal iliacus for inlet adduction. R PART resolves at this step.
layer2.pathology: Inhibited in L AIC (L posterior inlet in IS IR / nutation).

layer3.treatment: Step 3: L proximal iliacus + L glute med. HALT 2/5 → 3/5. R PART resolves (becomes negative). Exercises: R Sidelying Supported L Glute Med, R Sidelying Hemi 90-90 with L FA IR.

layer1.standard: Hip extension, lateral rotation, abduction (upper fibers), adduction (lower fibers). Primary hip extensor from flexed position.
layer1.actions: Hip extension, femoral lateral rotation, hip abduction (upper fibers), hip adduction (lower fibers).
layer1.movements: Hip extension, femoral ER, pelvic stabilization in stance.
layer1.pri: R glute max superior fibers → R IS IR (nutation, posterior inlet closing). Inferior fibers → R SI IR (posterior outlet closing). Key corrective in L AIC — works at both inlet and outlet.

layer2.laic: R glute max = corrective target. Facilitates R IS IR (inlet) + R SI IR (outlet). L glute max is compensatory in L AIC. Used in repositioning (L Sidelying R Glute Max) AND in Step 4.
layer2.pathology: L glute max compensatory/overactive in L AIC.

layer3.treatment: Step 4: R glute max + R piriformis + R coccygeus. HALT 4/5. L PADT resolves (becomes negative). Exercises: All Four R Glute Max, L Sidelying Supported R Glute Max with Hip Extension.
